title: Health & Emergencies
description: Describing illness, doctor visits, buying medicines, asking for help

lessons.title: Feeling Unwell
lessons.culturalNote: Describing general physical discomfort. In Marathi, body conditions are often described with 'malaa... hotay' (to me... is happening).

lessons.title: At the Doctor
lessons.culturalNote: Interacting with medical professionals. Explaining pain and symptoms.

phrases.english: I want to see the doctor

phrases.marathi: हे औषध दिवसातून तीन वेळा घ्या
phrases.transliteration: He aushadh divasaatoon teen veLaa ghyaa
phrases.english: Take this medicine three times a day

lessons.title: At the Pharmacy
lessons.culturalNote: Buying medicines (aushadh) and understanding dosage instructions.

phrases.english: What is the expiry date of this medicine?

lessons.title: Emergencies & Accidents
lessons.culturalNote: Calling emergency services or dealing with roadside accidents.

lessons.title: Asking for Help
lessons.culturalNote: Shouting for help or seeking assistance from passersby.

phrases.marathi: काळजी करू नका, मी मदत करतो
phrases.transliteration: KaaLji karoo nakaa, mee madat karto

title: Social Conversations
description: Weather talks, hobbies, inviting friends, small talk

lessons.title: Weather & Climate
lessons.culturalNote: Weather is a popular small talk topic. Maharashtra experiences heavy rains (monsoon) and scorching summers.

phrases.english: Clouds have gathered in the sky

lessons.title: Hobbies & Free Time
lessons.culturalNote: Sharing what you love to do in your leisure hours.

phrases.english: I like to listen to songs

lessons.title: Likes & Dislikes
lessons.culturalNote: Expressing preferences using 'aavaDtan' (liked) and 'aavaDat naahi' (disliked).

phrases.english: I like Puranpoli (sweet flatbread) very much

lessons.title: Inviting Friends
lessons.culturalNote: Inviting someone to your home or for an event. Hospitalities are key.

phrases.english: We are hosting a party on Sunday

lessons.title: Polite Small Talk
lessons.culturalNote: Essential phrases for maintaining a casual, polite conversation.

phrases.marathi: मला तुमची गप्पा मारून आनंद झाला
phrases.transliteration: Malaa tumchee gappaa maaroon aanand jhaalaa

title: Work & Business
description: Office communication, phone calls, meetings, appointments

lessons.title: In the Office
lessons.culturalNote: Typical workspace interactions and basic administrative needs.

phrases.english: There is a lot of work today

lessons.title: Professional Introductions
lessons.culturalNote: Introducing yourself in formal corporate or professional settings.

lessons.title: Phone Conversations
lessons.culturalNote: Answering calls, verifying caller identity, and taking messages.

lessons.title: Meetings & Discussions
lessons.culturalNote: Common corporate vocabulary for meetings and presentations.

lessons.title: Making Appointments
lessons.culturalNote: Scheduling business or formal meetings.

phrases.marathi: उद्या दुपारी दोन वाजता चालेल का?
phrases.transliteration: Udyaa dupaari don vaajataa chaalel ka?
phrases.english: Will tomorrow afternoon at 2 PM work?

title: Culture & Celebration
description: Festivals, blessings, hospitality customs, popular idioms

lessons.title: Traditional Festivals
lessons.culturalNote: Maharashtra celebrates Ganesh Utsav (Ganeshotsav) with massive grandeur. Diwali and Gudhi Padwa are also major.

lessons.title: Offering Blessings
lessons.culturalNote: Giving well-wishes or elder blessings, highly valued in Marathi homes.

phrases.english: Grow very big! (May you achieve success!)

lessons.title: Hospitality Customs
lessons.culturalNote: Maharashtrian guest etiquette: offering tea, sitting respectfully.

phrases.english: Please sit, will you have some tea?
Don't leave without eating a meal
Do come to our home for sure
Phone us once you reach home

lessons.title: Idioms & Cultural Sayings
lessons.culturalNote: Marathi is rich in 'mhani' (sayings/idioms) reflecting rustic wisdom and humor.

phrases.english: Excess of anything leads to ruin (lit: excess leads to soil/dust)
Between the devil and the deep blue sea (lit: ditch on one side, well on other)
As you sow, so shall you reap
Truth needs no proof (lit: why a mirror for a bangle on the hand?)
Forgetting the benefactor once the need is met (lit: once need ends, doctor dies)
A foolish adviser (lit: wise man sitting on a camel)
Distance lends enchantment to the view (lit: hills look beautiful from afar)

lessons.title: Congratulations
lessons.culturalNote: Celebrating achievements, promotions, or winning scores.

phrases.english: Sweeten your mouth (eat sweets to celebrate)
You did it! / You proved it!
To reach the peak of success
